result:
Report written to `/home/nate/charos/haros-events/jarvis-dup-verify/data-services-report.md`.

**Key finding: the duplicate-detection signal was a false positive.** The "host PIDs" cited in the brief (16133/16111/15585/15292) are container processes visible from the host's PID-namespace view. Verified via `/proc/<pid>/ns/pid` (each in a distinct container namespace, none in host's `4026532255`) and cross-checked with `docker top`. There are no host-native installs of n8n, gitea, meilisearch, or sftpgo on jarvis-wsl — `which` is empty, no systemd units, no host data dirs.

All four verdicts: **CONTAINER-ONLY** — nothing to retire. Volumes are tiny (24K/64K/140K) so very little real data has accumulated.

**Bonus finding worth flagging to Dad:** the `sftpgo` compose service declares no `volumes:` block at all (the `sftpgo-data` / `sftpgo-config` named volumes exist at the bottom of compose but aren't wired in). Any admin users / virtual folders / SFTP creds in family-filebrowser will be lost on next `docker rm`. Independent of this audit but worth fixing.
